title: Gödel, Escher, Bach
subtitle: An Eternal Golden Braid
author: Douglas R. Hofstadter
themes: Self-reference and strange loops are at the heart of consciousness, mathematics, and art
Gödel's incompleteness theorem shows that any sufficiently powerful formal system has true statements it cannot prove — a kind of self-reference
Escher's drawings and Bach's music explore similar patterns of recursion and self-reference
Meaning emerges from the interaction of levels in a system — from symbols to thoughts
The mind is a strange loop: a self-referential pattern that arises from the brain's activity
Artificial intelligence is possible in principle, but requires understanding the mechanisms of meaning and self-reference
excerpt: What is a self, and how can a self come out of stuff that is not self? Why are there selves at all? These are the questions that Gödel, Escher, Bach explores. The answer, I believe, lies in the concept of a 'strange loop' — a self-referential, tangled hierarchy that arises when a system loops back on itself. And the most beautiful strange loop of all is the human mind.
